AMD.reasoning: AMD is in a post-breakout consolidation rather than a pre-breakout squeeze, which is a marginal but viable setup for a continuation breakout. The consolidation range is bounded by support at $328.81 (hourly pivot, volume-confirmed, 6 tests) and resistance at $352.99 (hourly pivot high, volume-confirmed, 2 tests). The stock surged from ~$200 to $353 in the prior weeks, then pulled back to $310 and is now coiling in the $310-$353 range. A confirmed daily close above $352.99 with 1.5x+ daily volume would be a valid continuation breakout. RANGE BOUNDARIES: Support $328.81 / Resistance $352.99, range height ~$24. VOLUME RATIO: Daily relative volume is only 1.10x (below the 1.5x threshold required — NOT confirmed). Hourly relative volume is 1.91x (above threshold, suggesting intraday accumulation, which is an early signal but daily confirmation is still needed). SQUEEZE STATUS: Daily Bollinger width is 0.6396 — wide, meaning the big squeeze already fired and this is NOT a classic coiled setup at the daily level. Hourly BB width 0.2571 is more compressed, consistent with short-term consolidation. OVERBOUGHT RISK: Daily RSI-14 at 82.09 and RSI-21 at 85.78 are deeply overbought. Z-score 2.47 indicates statistical overextension. Wiki confirms this has been a persistent risk across multiple prior runs. ADX 73.57 confirms an extremely strong trend but also that the move is well-advanced. WIKI CONTEXT: Prior resistance levels at $268-$305 have been completely blown through. The prior entry zone ($241-$248) and stop ($270) are now deep historical levels — irrelevant to current action. The technicals wiki (last updated 2026-04-29, not stale) flags RSI overextension as the key risk. CONCLUSION: Setup is approaching but not yet confirmed. Entering NOW is premature — breakout requires a daily close above $352.99 with volume surge. Low confidence due to (1) no daily volume confirmation, (2) daily BB width already wide (squeeze done at daily level), (3) deeply overbought RSI. Signal is bullish lean only, pending confirmation.
